iterations.hypothesis: Use BTC/USDC (listing 1) microprice as the primary fair value reference for quoting BTC/USDE (listing 28). BTC/USDC has higher volume and should give a more reliable price signal. BTC/USDE spread volatility (EWMA-smoothed) sizes the half-spread. min_spread_bps=3.0 to cover the 1.2 bps maker fee on each side.
iterations.analysis: BTC/USDE trades $15-27 above BTC/USDC persistently (structural basis). Using USDC microprice as fair value caused our asks to land INSIDE the USDE spread when the basis was large — takers hit us because we were offering BTC/USDE below the USDE market price. Ask fills averaged -1.687 bps edge (selling below mid = adverse selection). All 128 fills occurred on May 13 00:00-12:31; zero on day 2 despite full USDE data. BTC/USDE typically trades at 0.125 bps spread (1 tick wide), so our min_spread_bps=3.0 quotes were normally far outside the market. Fills only happened during brief basis-driven windows where USDC reference put us inside USDE spread. PnL loss driven by: (a) selling below mid on adverse ask fills, (b) residual short position of -0.194 BTC with market rising from $80,459 to $81,043.
iterations.next_action: Switch to BTC/USDE own MicropriceFairValue (security_id=28) as the fair value reference to eliminate basis-driven adverse selection. Reduce min_spread_bps to 2.5 (covers 1.2 bps maker fee with 0.05 bps buffer per side) and use vol_alpha=0.9 for faster response to USDE spread changes. Keep USDC data available but use only for supplementary flow analysis in later iterations.
